preamble: The shift from a lateral affricate [tɬ] to a postalveolar fricative [ʃ] represents a complex weakening and shifting of the place of articulation. This often occurs through a stage of spirantization where the lateral component is lost or transformed into a sibilant fricative.

examples.note: In some peripheral dialects of Nahuatl, historical [tɬ] has shifted toward [ʃ] or [s] in specific environments.
Lateral affricates may undergo weakening in rapid speech or specific morphophonemic contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
